zh.lifeSummary: 當Audebert II de la Marche於1020年出生在法國利姆辛可斯拉 馬時，他的父親Bernard I Comte de la Marche28歲，母親Amélie d'Aulney, Comtesse de La Marche20歲。他於大約1057年與Ponce Lusignan在法國諾門迪結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1088年在法國阿奎泰吉洛德拉 馬享年68歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Audebert II de la Marche于1020年出生在法国利姆辛可斯拉 馬时，他的父亲Bernard I Comte de la Marche28岁，母亲Amélie d'Aulney, Comtesse de La Marche20岁。他于大约1057年与Ponce Lusignan在法国諾門迪结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1088年在法国阿奎泰吉洛德拉 馬享年68岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Marche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   March  .

variants.name: March
meanings: English: habitational name from March (Cambridgeshire) or from any of numerous minor English placenames deriving from Middle English marche ‘boundary’ (partly from Old English mearc in the dative-case form mearce, partly from Old French marche). Compare Mark 2.
Catalan: from the personal name March, Catalan equivalent of Mark 1. This surname is also found in southern France.
German: topographic name from Middle Low German march, Middle High German marc(h) ‘mark, sign, boundary’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word (Bavaria).

nameMeanings.name: Audebert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lorette, Philippe.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Aldeberht, composed of the elements ald ‘old’ and berht ‘bright’.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Audebert II de la Marche was born in 1020, in La Marche, Creuse, Limousin, France, his father, Bernard I Comte de la Marche, was 28 and his mother, Amélie d'Aulney, Comtesse de La Marche, was 20. He married Ponce Lusignan about 1057, in Normandy, France. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. He died in 1088, in La Marche, Gironde, Aquitaine, France, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Audebert II de la Marche est né en 1020, à La Marche, Creuse, Limousin, France, son père, Bernard I Comte de la Marche, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Amélie d'Aulney, Comtesse de La Marche, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Ponce Lusignan environ 1057, à Normandie, France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé en 1088, à La Marche, Gironde, Aquitaine, France, à l’âge de 68 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Audebert II de la Marche 1020 in La Marche, Creuse, Limousin, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Bernard I Comte de la Marche 28 und seine Mutter Amélie d'Aulney, Comtesse de La Marche 20. Er hat um 1057 in Normandie, Frankreich Ponce Lusignan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb 1088 im Alter von 68 Jahren in La Marche, Gironde, Aquitaine, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Audebert II de la Marche è nato il nel 1020 nel La Marche, Creuse, Limousin, Francia, il suo padre, Bernard I Comte de la Marche, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Amélie d'Aulney, Comtesse de La Marche, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Ponce Lusignan circa 1057, nel Normandie, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. Morì nel 1088, nel La Marche, Gironde, Aquitaine, Francia, all'età di 68 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Audebert II de la Marcheが1020年にフランスリモウシンクレウゼラ マルチェで生まれた時，父Bernard I Comte de la Marcheは28歳，母Amélie d'Aulney, Comtesse de La Marcheは20歳であった。約1057年にフランスノーマンディでPonce Lusignanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1088年にフランスアクイティアインギロンドラ マルチェで68歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Audebert II de la Marche(이)가 1020년 프랑스 리모우신 크레우스 라 마체에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Bernard I Comte de la Marche(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Amélie d'Aulney, Comtesse de La Marche(은)는 20세였다. 그는 대략 1057년 프랑스 노먼디에서 Ponce Lusignan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1088년 프랑스 아키테인 지론드 라 마체에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Audebert II de la Marche nasceu em 1020, em La Marche, Creuse, Limousin, França, seu pai, Bernard I Comte de la Marche, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Amélie d'Aulney, Comtesse de La Marche, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Ponce Lusignan aproximadamente 1057, em Normandie, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1088, em La Marche, Gironde, Aquitaine, França, com 68 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Audebert II de la Marche родился в 1020 г. в местности ла марч, креус, лимусин, Франция, его отцу по имени Bernard I Comte de la Marche было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Amélie d'Aulney, Comtesse de La Marche, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Ponce Lusignan около 1057 г. в местности норманди, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер в 1088 г. в местности ла марч, гиронд, аквитеин, Франция в возрасте 68 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Audebert II de la Marche nació en 1020, en La Marche, Creuse, Limousin, Francia, su papá, Bernard I Comte de la Marche, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Amélie d'Aulney, Comtesse de La Marche, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Ponce Lusignan aproximadamente 1057, en Normandie, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió en 1088, en La Marche, Gironde, Aquitaine, Francia, a la edad de 68 años.
